defs: Any one of numerous species of small passerine birds native of tropical America. See Banana quit, under Banana, and Guitguit.

records.ety: [OE. quite, OF. quite, F. quitte. See Quit, v., Quiet.]

defs: Released from obligation, charge, penalty, etc.; free; clear; absolved; acquitted.
quotes: The owner of the ox shall be quit.
notes: ☞ This word is sometimes used in the form quits, colloquially; as, to be quits with one, that is, to have made mutual satisfaction of demands with him; to be even with him; hence, as an exclamation: Quits! we are even, or on equal terms. “To cry quits with the commons in their complaints.”

records.ety: [OE. quiten, OF. quiter, quitier, cuitier, F. quitter, to acquit, quit, LL. quietare, fr. L. quietare to calm, to quiet, fr. quietus quiet. See Quiet, a., and cf. Quit, a., Quite, Acquit, Requite.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Quit or Quitted; p. pr. & vb. n. Quitting.]
usage: -- Quit, Leave. Leave is a general term, signifying merely an act of departure; quit implies a going without intention of return, a final and absolute abandonment.

defs: To set at rest; to free, as from anything harmful or oppressive; to relieve; to clear; to liberate.
quotes: To quit you of this fear, you have already looked Death in the face; what have you found so terrible in it?

defs: To release from obligation, accusation, penalty, or the like; to absolve; to acquit.
quotes: There may no gold them quyte.
God will relent, and quit thee all his debt.

defs: To discharge, as an obligation or duty; to meet and satisfy, as a claim or debt; to make payment for or of; to requite; to repay.
quotes: The blissful martyr quyte you your meed.
Enkindle all the sparks of nature To quit this horrid act.
Before that judge that quits each soul his hire.

defs: To meet the claims upon, or expectations entertained of; to conduct; to acquit; -- used reflexively.
quotes: Be strong, and quit yourselves like men.
Samson hath quit himself Like Samson.

defs: To carry through; to go through to the end.
quotes: Never worthy prince a day did quit With greater hazard and with more renown.

defs: To have done with; to cease from; to stop; hence, to depart from; to leave; to forsake; as, to quit work; to quit the place; to quit jesting.
quotes: Such a superficial way of examining is to quit truth for appearance.
Does not the earth quit scores with all the elements in the noble fruits that issue from it?

defs: To go away; to depart; to stop doing a thing; to cease.
